title: Using a default `ARG` as a parameter for `COPY --from=` doesn't use previously defined stage
body:
### Issue Description

Using a default `ARG` as a parameter for `COPY --from=` doesn't use previously defined stage and instead asks to select a registry to retrieve the image from.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Use the following `Containerfile`:

```docker
FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest AS build
RUN echo "build" >> /variant

FROM build AS dev
RUN echo "build:dev" >> /variant

FROM build AS staging
RUN echo "build:staging" >> /variant

FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest
ARG VARIANT="staging"
COPY --from="${VARIANT}" /variant /variant
RUN echo "${VARIANT}" >> /variant
```

Run `podman build --no-cache --tag variant-test "$PWD" && podman run --rm variant-test cat /variant`.

### Describe the results you received

Podman asks to select a registry to retrieve the `staging` image from.

### Describe the results you expected

The image should be built with the previously declared `staging` stage as when `--build-arg` is specified.

### podman info output

```yaml
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      5.6.0
API Version:  5.6.0
Go Version:   go1.25.7 (Red Hat 1.25.7-1.el9_7)
Built:        Fri Feb 20 18:49:57 2026
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

host:
  arch: amd64
  buildahVersion: 1.41.8
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

No

### Additional environment details

RHEL 9.7 on bare metal.

### Additional information

Using the same `Containerfile`, the build works as expected when specifying `--build-arg` like the following:

* `podman build --no-cache --tag variant-test --build-arg VARIANT="dev" "$PWD" && podman run --rm variant-test cat /variant`
* `podman build --no-cache --tag variant-test --build-arg VARIANT="staging" "$PWD" && podman run --rm variant-test cat /variant`

comments.body:
This is not a bug; variable expansion is not supported for `--from`. You might have to resort to something like
```
ARG VARIANT="staging"

FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest AS build
RUN echo "build" >> /variant

FROM build AS dev
RUN echo "build:dev" >> /variant

FROM build AS staging
RUN echo "build:staging" >> /variant

FROM ${VARIANT} as selected

FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest
COPY --from=selected /variant /variant
RUN echo "${VARIANT}" >> /variant
```

EDIT: saw your comment re: `--build-arg`, it seems as if podman _might_ be able to support default ARG, but it doesn't, so there's an inconsistency. 

@Luap99 It looks like we _can_ support in-line ARG defaults as well?

Moved to buildah

I leave it to @nalind to decide whether this is a dup or not.

The example appears to build successfully if we set `--skip-unused-stages=false`, which suggests a bug in how that pruning is done.  @Honny1 do you have some cycles to look at that part?

I don't think this is a duplicate of #4820, which was fixed, but fell off the radar and wasn't closed.

<!-- Answer -->
This issue was [previously fixed](https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/4573) in buildah 1.28.2, and you're on 1.41.8 which should have that fix. However, this behavior can still occur due to ARG processing timing issues.

**Recommended workaround**: Declare the ARG *before* the first FROM statement (as a global ARG), then redeclare it in the final stage:

```dockerfile
ARG VARIANT="staging"

FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest AS build
RUN echo "build" >> /variant

FROM build AS dev
RUN echo "build:dev" >> /variant

FROM build AS staging
RUN echo "build:staging" >> /variant

FROM docker.io/library/busybox:latest
ARG VARIANT
COPY --from="${VARIANT}" /variant /variant
RUN echo "${VARIANT}" >> /variant
```

Global ARGs (declared before first FROM) are [processed earlier and made available before COPY --from validation](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/79074e897647bf297cd7025cc05c16ca53f3184e/vendor/github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/builder.go#L656-L664), which bypasses the timing issue you're encountering.

If this workaround doesn't help, this may be a regression worth investigating further—the core [fix for COPY --from with ARG variables](https://github.com/containers/buildah/commit/44614d9fe80820f8abb7b8411bc40fa7c516e52b) is still present in the 1.41.x codebase.

Dosu was misled by a similar issue. But issue was very similar. I don't know if that would help me.
